text: Highlight Clip Michael Saylor: Bitcoin Needs Great Products to Tap Into 99% of Capital Strategy founder Michael Saylor said in a June 17 interview with BTCPrague that promoting Bitcoin cannot rely only on preaching its asset properties. He compared it to aluminum: the way to sell aluminum is not to say it is lighter than steel, but to build airplanes and sell tickets, letting people buy aluminum through products they use. Saylor said Bitcoin also needs to be embedded in compelling digital products and connected to global capital markets to reach billions of users and companies. "They have 99% of the money," he said. "They're not coming to us. We need to go to them." — link

text: Photonics Stack! Let's Unpack It Mapping the the sector's value chain and choke points From foundational materials and epitaxy tools to optical devices, manufacturing, testing, and final connectivity systems powering AI infrastructure —— Short TLDR to understand Photonics more • What is photonics: Using light (photons) instead of electricity to move insane amounts of data very fast with way less power and heat • Why needed: AI data centers are hitting its limit - wires can't keep up with GPU clusters without melting everything or costing a fortune in electricity • How does it work: Lasers make light that carries data, tiny waveguides guide the data on the chip, modulators encode the info, and detectors turn it back to electricity. Co-packaged optics puts the light stuff right next to the processors • When: Pumping now as AI capex at ATH and NVIDIA dropped billions into it to scale • Where: Mostly found inside massive AI data centers plus some telecom and future LiDAR/self-driving stuff

text: How MEV Bot "JaredFromSubway" got exploited for $15m TLDR • JaredFromSubway is a famous automated MEV bot that makes money by doing "sandwich attacks" on other people's trades on DEXes. It has made millions by front-running and back-running trades • The attacker created 66 fake token contracts that looked exactly like real popular tokensa + fake liquidity pools on DEXes that appeared to offer very profitable arbitrage or trading opportunities • JaredFromSubway found these fake pools and thought they were great deals worth trading on. The bot started with small transactions and as part of normal trading, it automatically gave small approvals (permissions) to attacker-controlled helper contracts to spend its real tokens • These small approvals were used during the fake trades, so everything looked normal. The bot made some profit or saw no red flags • The bot increased its activity with these routes and gave much larger approvals to the same attacker-controlled contracts • These big approvals were not fully used up and not revoked. They remained active on the real token contracts (WETH, USDC, USDT) • With the approvals in place, the attacker didn't need to hack the bot's code or bypass its security. They simply used their own smart contract to call transferFrom on the real token contracts • The bot was drained of roughly $15 million

text: Japan Corporate Pension Fund to Allocate 1% of Assets to Crypto in FY2026 Japan's National Commercial Enterprise Pension Fund plans to begin investing in cryptocurrencies in fiscal 2026, with crypto expected to account for about 1% of its total assets. The fund covers around 1,200 SMEs and manages about 21.3 billion yen, or $136 million. It reportedly plans to invest through a passive multi-crypto fund managed by a major hedge fund, mainly to diversify currency risk. — link

text: Highlight Clip Ripple CEO Blasts JPMorgan: Intentional misrepresentation to protect profits On June 11, 2026, Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse responded to JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon's opposition to the Clarity Act during an interview on Fox Business. Garlinghouse stated that Dimon has been dismissing the crypto industry for decades, calling it a Ponzi scheme and Bitcoin a "pet rock." He believes that since JPMorgan generates $20 billion in revenue and over $5 billion in profit from its payments business, Dimon's attempt to block the bill is essentially to dig a deeper moat to protect this highly profitable business and maintain the status quo. Furthermore, Garlinghouse believes Dimon's claim that the bill would reduce compliance requirements is completely untrue, characterizing it as either an intentional misrepresentation or highly negligent to make support for the act go away. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Pump fun's New Crypto Bounty Feature Under Fire Over Humiliating and Risky Stunts According to the New York Post, meme coin launchpad Pump fun has drawn criticism over its new bounty feature, which allows users to post tasks and reward participants with crypto. Since launching on June 4, the feature has paid out more than $370,000, with around 270 open bounties still offering over $200,000 in rewards. Some tasks reportedly involve forehead tattoos, putting one's face in a toilet, quitting a job on camera, and even climbing Mount Everest. One man in the Philippines earned $15,000 in crypto for tattooing "bounty fun" on his forehead. The feature has also drawn criticism from New York Governor Kathy Hochul and X head of product Nikita Bier. — link
